content.problemStatement:
Chef is running a lemonade stand, and each lemonade costs him Rs. XXX to make.

Chef will have NNN total customers coming to his lemonade stand today, with the iii-th customer willing to spend Rs. iii on a lemonade.

So, the first customer is willing to spend Rs. 111, the second Rs. 222, the third Rs. 333 and so on.

For each customer, Chef can choose whether to accept and provide him with lemonade (at the amount he is willing to pay), or reject him. Find the maximum profit Chef can make. Ignore any other costs.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and XXX.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum profit Chef can make.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef chooses to accept the second, third, fourth and fifth customers. His total cost of making the lemonade would be 2⋅4=82 \cdot 4 = 82⋅4=8, and he gets 2+3+4+5=142 + 3 + 4 + 5 = 142+3+4+5=14 for a profit of 14−8=614-8=614−8=6.
Test case 222: Chef only accepts the fifth customer.
